steps.title: Find total composition from muscle percentage
steps.content:
We know muscle is 133 kg and comprises 65.9% of the buffalo's total composition. Using the percentage formula:
$$\text{Total} = \frac{133}{0.659} \approx 201.82 \text{ kg}$$

steps.title: Calculate the remaining composition
steps.content:
Since muscle makes up 65.9%, the remaining composition (bone and fat combined) is 100% - 65.9% = 34.1% of the total:
$$\text{Remaining} = 201.82 \times 0.341 \approx 68.82 \text{ kg}$$

steps.title: Find bone mass from the remaining composition
steps.content:
Of the remaining composition, 46.2% is bone:
$$\text{Bone} = 68.82 \times 0.462 \approx 31.80 \text{ kg}$$
Rounded to the nearest tenth: 31.8 kg
